verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचब्रह्मन् वेदितुमिच्छाम: स्वसारां रामकृष्णयो: ।यथोपयेमे विजयो या ममासीत् पितामही ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācabrahman veditum icchāmaḥsvasārāṁ rāma-kṛṣṇayoḥyathopayeme vijayoyā mamāsīt pitāmahī
verses.text: King Parīkṣit said: O brāhmaṇa, we would like to learn how Arjuna married Lord Balarāma’s and Lord Kṛṣṇa’s sister, who was my grandmother.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचअर्जुनस्तीर्थयात्रायां पर्यटन्नवनीं प्रभु: ।गत: प्रभासमश‍ृणोन्मातुलेयीं स आत्मन: ॥ २ ॥दुर्योधनाय रामस्तां दास्यतीति न चापरे ।तल्लिप्सु: स यतिर्भूत्वा त्रिदण्डी द्वारकामगात् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaarjunas tīrtha-yātrāyāṁparyaṭann avanīṁ prabhuḥgataḥ prabhāsam aśṛṇonmātuleyīṁ sa ātmanaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: While traveling far and wide visiting various holy places of pilgrimage, Arjuna came to Prabhāsa. There he heard that Lord Balarāma intended to give his maternal cousin Subhadrā to Duryodhana in marriage, and that no one else approved of this plan. Arjuna wanted to marry her himself, so he disguised himself as a renunciant, complete with triple staff, and went to Dvārakā.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र वै वार्षितान् मासानवात्सीत् स्वार्थसाधक: ।पौरै: सभाजितोऽभीक्ष्णं रामेणाजानता च स: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra vai vārṣitān māsānavātsīt svārtha-sādhakaḥpauraiḥ sabhājito ’bhīkṣṇaṁrāmeṇājānatā ca saḥ
verses.text: He stayed there during the monsoon months to fulfill his purpose. Lord Balarāma and the other residents of the city, not recognizing him, offered him all honor and hospitality.

verses.sanskrit: एकदा गृहमानीय आतिथ्येन निमन्‍त्र्य तम् ।श्रद्धयोपहृतं भैक्ष्यं बलेन बुभुजे किल ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ekadā gṛham ānīyaātithyena nimantrya tamśraddhayopahṛtaṁ bhaikṣyaṁbalena bubhuje kila
verses.text: One day Lord Balarāma brought him to His home as His invited dinner guest, and Arjuna ate the food the Lord respectfully offered him.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽपश्यत्तत्र महतीं कन्यां वीरमनोहराम् ।प्रीत्युत्फुल्ल‍ेक्षणस्तस्यां भावक्षुब्धं मनो दधे ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’paśyat tatra mahatīṁkanyāṁ vīra-mano-harāmprīty-utphullekṣaṇas tasyāṁbhāva-kṣubdhaṁ mano dadhe
verses.text: There he saw the wonderful maiden Subhadrā, who was enchanting to heroes. His eyes opened wide with delight, and his mind became agitated and absorbed in thoughts of her.

verses.sanskrit: सापि तं चकमे वीक्ष्य नारीणां हृदयंगमम् ।हसन्ती व्रीडितापाङ्गी तन्न्यस्तहृदयेक्षणा ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sāpi taṁ cakame vīkṣyanārīṇāṁ hṛdayaṁ-gamamhasantī vrīḍitāpaṅgītan-nyasta-hṛdayekṣaṇā
verses.text: Arjuna was very attractive to women, and as soon as Subhadrā saw him, she wanted to have him as her husband. Smiling bashfully with sidelong glances, she fixed her heart and eyes upon him.

verses.sanskrit: तां परं समनुध्यायन्नन्तरं प्रेप्सुरर्जुन: ।न लेभे शं भ्रमच्चित्त: कामेनातिबलीयसा ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁ paraṁ samanudhyāyannantaraṁ prepsur arjunaḥna lebhe śaṁ bhramac-cittaḥkāmenāti-balīyasā
verses.text: Meditating only on her and waiting for the opportunity to take her away, Arjuna had no peace. His heart trembled with passionate desire.

verses.sanskrit: महत्यां देवयात्रायां रथस्थां दुर्गनिर्गताम् ।जहारानुमत: पित्रो: कृष्णस्य च महारथ: ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: mahatyāṁ deva-yātrāyāṁratha-sthāṁ durga-nirgatāṁjahārānumataḥ pitroḥkṛṣṇasya ca mahā-rathaḥ
verses.text: Once, on the occasion of a great temple festival in honor of the Supreme Lord, Subhadrā rode out of the fortresslike palace on a chariot, and at that time the mighty chariot warrior Arjuna took the opportunity to kidnap her. Subhadrā’s parents and Kṛṣṇa had sanctioned this.

verses.sanskrit: रथस्थो धनुरादाय शूरांश्चारुन्धतो भटान् ।विद्राव्य क्रोशतां स्वानां स्वभागं मृगराडिव ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: ratha-stho dhanur ādāyaśūrāṁś cārundhato bhaṭānvidrāvya krośatāṁ svānāṁsva-bhāgaṁ mṛga-rāḍ iva
verses.text: Standing on his chariot, Arjuna took up his bow and drove off the valiant fighters and palace guards who tried to block his way. As her relatives shouted in anger, he took Subhadrā away just as a lion takes his prey from the midst of lesser animals.

verses.sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा क्षुभितो राम: पर्वणीव महार्णव: ।गृहीतपाद: कृष्णेन सुहृद्भ‍िश्चानुसान्‍त्‍वित: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: tac chrutvā kṣubhito rāmaḥparvaṇīva mahārṇavaḥgṛhīta-pādaḥ kṛṣṇenasuhṛdbhiś cānusāntvitaḥ
verses.text: When He heard of Subhadrā’s kidnapping, Lord Balarāma became as disturbed as the ocean during the full moon, but Lord Kṛṣṇa respectfully took hold of His feet and, together with other family members, pacified Him by explaining the matter.

verses.sanskrit: प्राहिणोत् पारिबर्हाणि वरवध्वोर्मुदा बल: ।महाधनोपस्करेभरथाश्वनरयोषित: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāhiṇot pāribarhāṇivara-vadhvor mudā balaḥmahā-dhanopaskarebha-rathāśva-nara-yoṣitaḥ
verses.text: Lord Balarāma then happily sent the bride and groom very valuable wedding gifts consisting of elephants, chariots, horses and male and female servants.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचकृष्णस्यासीद् द्विजश्रेष्ठ: श्रुतदेव इति श्रुत: ।कृष्णैकभक्त्या पूर्णार्थ: शान्त: कविरलम्पट: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācakṛṣṇasyāsīd dvija-śreṣṭhaḥśrutadeva iti śrutaḥkṛṣṇaika-bhaktyā pūrṇārthaḥśāntaḥ kavir alampataḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: There was a devotee of Kṛṣṇa’s known as Śrutadeva, who was a first-class brāhmaṇa. Perfectly satisfied by rendering unalloyed devotional service to Lord Kṛṣṇa, he was peaceful, learned and free from sense gratification.

verses.sanskrit: स उवास विदेहेषु मिथिलायां गृहाश्रमी ।अनीहयागताहार्यनिर्वर्तितनिजक्रिय: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa uvāsa videheṣumithilāyāṁ gṛhāśramīanīhayāgatāhārya-nirvartita-nija-kriyaḥ
verses.text: Living as a religious householder in the city of Mithilā, within the kingdom of Videha, he managed to fulfill his obligations while maintaining himself with whatever sustenance easily came his way.

verses.sanskrit: यात्रामात्रं त्वहरहर्दैवादुपनमत्युत ।नाधिकं तावता तुष्ट: क्रिया चक्रे यथोचिता: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yātrā-mātraṁ tv ahar ahardaivād upanamaty utanādhikaṁ tāvatā tuṣṭaḥkriyā cakre yathocitāḥ
verses.text: By the will of Providence he obtained each day just what he needed for his maintenance, and no more. Satisfied with this much, he properly executed his religious duties.

verses.sanskrit: तथा तद्राष्ट्रपालोऽङ्ग बहुलाश्व इति श्रुत: ।मैथिलो निरहम्मान उभावप्यच्युतप्रियौ ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathā tad-rāṣṭra-pālo ’ṅgabahulāśva iti śrutaḥmaithilo niraham-mānaubhāv apy acyuta-priyau
verses.text: Similarly free from false ego was the ruler of that kingdom, my dear Parīkṣit, a descendant of the Mithila dynasty named Bahulāśva. Both these devotees were very dear to Lord Acyuta.

verses.sanskrit: तयो: प्रसन्नो भगवान् दारुकेणाहृतं रथम् ।आरुह्य साकं मुनिभिर्विदेहान् प्रययौ प्रभु: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tayoḥ prasanno bhagavāndārukeṇāhṛtaṁ rathamāruhya sākaṁ munibhirvidehān prayayau prabhuḥ
verses.text: Pleased with both of them, the Supreme Personality of Godhead mounted His chariot, which Dāruka had brought, and traveled to Videha with a group of sages.

verses.sanskrit: नारदो वामदेवोऽत्रि: कृष्णो रामोऽसितोऽरुणि: ।अहं बृहस्पति: कण्वो मैत्रेयश्‍च्यवनादय: ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: nārado vāmadevo ’triḥkṛṣṇo rāmo ’sito ’ruṇiḥahaṁ bṛhaspatiḥ kaṇvomaitreyaś cyavanādayaḥ
verses.text: Among these sages were Nārada, Vāmadeva, Atri, Kṛṣṇa-dvaipāyana Vyāsa, Paraśurāma, Asita, Aruṇi, myself, Bṛhaspati, Kaṇva, Maitreya and Cyavana.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र तत्र तमायान्तं पौरा जानपदा नृप ।उपतस्थु: सार्घ्यहस्ता ग्रहै: सूर्यमिवोदितम् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra tatra tam āyāntaṁpaurā jānapadā nṛpaupatasthuḥ sārghya-hastāgrahaiḥ sūryam ivoditam
verses.text: In every city and town the Lord passed along the way, O King, the people came forward to worship Him with offerings of arghya water in their hands, as if to worship the risen sun surrounded by planets.

verses.sanskrit: आनर्तधन्वकुरुजाङ्गलकङ्कमत्स्य-पाञ्चालकुन्तिमधुकेकयकोशलार्णा: ।अन्ये च तन्मुखसरोजमुदारहास-स्‍निग्धेक्षणं नृप पपुर्द‍ृशिभिर्नृनार्य: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: ānarta-dhanva-kuru-jāṅgala-kaṅka-matsya-pāñcāla-kunti-madhu-kekaya-kośalārṇāḥanye ca tan-mukha-sarojam udāra-hāsa-snigdhekṣaṇaṁ nṛpa papur dṛśibhir nr-nāryaḥ
verses.text: The men and women of Ānarta, Dhanva, Kuru-jāṅgala, Kaṅka, Matsya, Pañcāla, Kunti, Madhu, Kekaya, Kośala, Arṇa and many other kingdoms drank with their eyes the nectarean beauty of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s lotuslike face, which was graced with generous smiles and affectionate glances.

verses.sanskrit: तेभ्य: स्ववीक्षणविनष्टतमिस्रद‍ृग्भ्य:क्षेमं त्रिलोकगुरुरर्थद‍ृशं च यच्छन् ।श‍ृण्वन् दिगन्तधवलं स्वयशोऽशुभघ्नंगीतं सुरैर्नृभिरगाच्छनकैर्विदेहान् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tebhyaḥ sva-vīkṣaṇa-vinaṣṭa-tamisra-dṛgbhyaḥkṣemaṁ tri-loka-gurur artha-dṛśaṁ ca yacchanśṛṇvan dig-anta-dhavalaṁ sva-yaśo ’śubha-ghnaṁgītaṁ surair nṛbhir agāc chanakair videhān
verses.text: Simply by glancing at those who came to see Him, Lord Kṛṣṇa, the spiritual master of the three worlds, delivered them from the blindness of materialism. As He thus endowed them with fearlessness and divine vision, He heard demigods and men singing His glories, which purify the entire universe and destroy all misfortune. Gradually, He reached Videha.

verses.sanskrit: तेऽच्युतं प्राप्तमाकर्ण्य पौरा जानपदा नृप ।अभीयुर्मुदितास्तस्मै गृहीतार्हणपाणय: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: te ’cyutaṁ prāptam ākarṇyapaurā jānapadā nṛpaabhīyur muditās tasmaigṛhītārhaṇa-pāṇayaḥ
verses.text: Hearing that Lord Acyuta had arrived, O King, the residents of the cities and villages of Videha joyfully came forth to receive Him with offerings in their hands.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा त उत्तम:श्लोकं प्रीत्युत्फुलाननाशया: ।कैर्धृताञ्जलिभिर्नेमु: श्रुतपूर्वांस्तथा मुनीन् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā ta uttamaḥ-ślokaṁprīty-utphulānanāśayāḥkair dhṛtāñjalibhir nemuḥśruta-pūrvāṁs tathā munīn
verses.text: As soon as the people saw Lord Uttamaḥśloka, their faces and hearts blossomed with affection. Joining their palms above their heads, they bowed down to the Lord and to the sages accompanying Him, whom they had previously only heard about.

verses.sanskrit: स्वानुग्रहाय सम्प्राप्तं मन्वानौ तं जगद्गुरुम् ।मैथिल: श्रुतदेवश्च पादयो: पेततु: प्रभो: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: svānugrahāya samprāptaṁmanvānau taṁ jagad-gurummaithilaḥ śrutadevaś capādayoḥ petatuḥ prabhoḥ
verses.text: Both the King of Mithilā and Śrutadeva fell at the Lord’s feet, each thinking that the spiritual master of the universe had come there just to show him mercy.

verses.sanskrit: न्यमन्त्रयेतां दाशार्हमातिथ्येन सह द्विजै: ।मैथिल: श्रुतदेवश्च युगपत् संहताञ्जली ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: nyamantrayetāṁ dāśārhamātithyena saha dvijaiḥmaithilaḥ śrutadevaś cayugapat saṁhatāñjalī
verses.text: At exactly the same time, King Maithila and Śrutadeva each went forward with joined palms and invited the Lord of the Daśārhas to be his guest, along with the brāhmaṇa sages.

verses.sanskrit: भगवांस्तदभिप्रेत्य द्वयो: प्रियचिकीर्षया ।उभयोराविशद् गेहमुभाभ्यां तदलक्षित: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavāṁs tad abhipretyadvayoḥ priya-cikīrṣayāubhayor āviśad gehamubhābhyāṁ tad-alakṣitaḥ
verses.text: Wanting to please them both, the Lord accepted both their invitations. Thus He simultaneously went to both homes, and neither could see Him entering the other’s house.

verses.sanskrit: श्रान्तानप्यथ तान् दूराज्जनक: स्वगृहागतान् ।आनीतेष्वासनाग्र्‍येषु सुखासीनान् महामना: ॥ २७ ॥प्रवृद्धभक्त्या उद्धर्षहृदयास्राविलेक्षण: ।नत्वा तदङ्‍‍घ्रीन् प्रक्षाल्य तदपो लोकपावनी: ॥ २८ ॥सकुटुम्बो वहन् मूर्ध्ना पूजयां चक्र ईश्वरान् ।गन्धमाल्याम्बराकल्पधूपदीपार्घ्यगोवृषै: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrāntān apy atha tān dūrājjanakaḥ sva-gṛhāgatānānīteṣv āsanāgryeṣusukhāsīnān mahā-manāḥ
verses.text: When King Bahulāśva, a descendant of Janaka, saw Lord Kṛṣṇa approaching his house from a distance with the sages, who were somewhat fatigued from the journey, he immediately arranged to have seats of honor brought out for them. After they were all comfortably seated, the wise King, his heart overflowing with joy and his eyes clouded by tears, bowed down to them and washed their feet with intense devotion. Taking the wash water, which could purify the entire world, he sprinkled it on his head and the heads of his family members. Then he worshiped all those great lords by offering them fragrant sandalwood paste, flower garlands, fine clothing and ornaments, incense, lamps, arghya and cows and bulls.

verses.sanskrit: वाचा मधुरया प्रीणन्निदमाहान्नतर्पितान् ।पादावङ्कगतौ विष्णो: संस्पृशञ्छनकैर्मुदा ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: vācā madhurayā prīṇannidam āhānna-tarpitānpādāv aṅka-gatau viṣṇoḥsaṁspṛśañ chanakair mudā
verses.text: When they had eaten to their full satisfaction, for their further pleasure the King began to speak slowly and in a gentle voice as he held Lord Viṣṇu’s feet in his lap and happily massaged them.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीबहुलाश्‍व उवाचभवान् हि सर्वभूतानामात्मा साक्षी स्वद‍ृग् विभो ।अथ नस्त्वत्पदाम्भोजं स्मरतां दर्शनं गत: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bahulāśva uvācabhavān hi sarva-bhūtānāmātmā sākṣī sva-dṛg vibhoatha nas tvat-padāmbhojaṁsmaratāṁ darśanaṁ gataḥ
verses.text: Śrī Bahulāśva said: O almighty Lord, You are the Soul of all created beings, their self-illumined witness, and now You are giving Your audience to us, who constantly meditate on Your lotus feet.

verses.sanskrit: स्ववचस्तद‍ृतं कर्तुमस्मद्‌दृग्गोचरो भवान् ।यदात्थैकान्तभक्तान् मे नानन्त: श्रीरज: प्रिय: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sva-vacas tad ṛtaṁ kartumasmad-dṛg-gocaro bhavānyad ātthaikānta-bhaktān menānantaḥ śrīr ajaḥ priyaḥ
verses.text: You have said, “Neither Ananta, Goddess Śrī nor unborn Brahmā is dearer to Me than My unalloyed devotee.” To prove Your own words true, You have now revealed Yourself to our eyes.

verses.sanskrit: को नु त्वच्चरणाम्भोजमेवंविद् विसृजेत् पुमान् ।निष्किञ्चनानां शान्तानां मुनीनां यस्त्वमात्मद: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ko nu tvac-caraṇāmbhojamevaṁ-vid visṛjet pumānniṣkiñcanānāṁ śāntānāṁmunīnāṁ yas tvam ātma-daḥ
verses.text: What person who knows this truth would ever abandon Your lotus feet, when You are ready to give Your very self to peaceful sages who call nothing their own?

verses.sanskrit: योऽवतीर्य यदोर्वंशे नृणां संसरतामिह ।यशो वितेने तच्छान्त्यै त्रैलोक्यवृजिनापहम् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yo ’vatīrya yador vaṁśenṛṇāṁ saṁsaratām ihayaśo vitene tac-chāntyaitrai-lokya-vṛjināpaham
verses.text: Appearing in the Yadu dynasty, You have spread Your glories, which can remove all the sins of the three worlds, just to deliver those entrapped in the cycle of birth and death.

verses.sanskrit: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते कृष्णायाकुण्ठमेधसे ।नारायणाय ऋषये सुशान्तं तप ईयुषे ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: namas tubhyaṁ bhagavatekṛṣṇāyākuṇṭha-medhasenārāyaṇāya ṛṣayesu-śāntaṁ tapa īyuṣe
verses.text: Obeisances to You, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose intelligence is ever unrestricted. Obeisances to the sage Nara-Nārāyaṇa, who always undergoes austerities in perfect peace.

verses.sanskrit: दिनानि कतिचिद् भूमन् गृहान् नो निवस द्विजै: ।समेत: पादरजसा पुनीहीदं निमे: कुलम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: dināni katicid bhūmangṛhān no nivasa dvijaiḥsametaḥ pāda-rajasāpunīhīdaṁ nimeḥ kulam
verses.text: Please stay a few days in our house, along with these brāhmaṇas, O all-pervading one, and with the dust of Your feet sanctify this dynasty of Nimi.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युपामन्त्रितो राज्ञा भगवाल्ँ लोकभावन: ।उवास कुर्वन् कल्याणं मिथिलानरयोषिताम् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity upāmantrito rājñābhagavāḻ loka-bhāvanaḥuvāsa kurvan kalyāṇaṁmithilā-nara-yoṣitām
verses.text: [Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued:] Thus invited by the King, the Supreme Lord, sustainer of the world, consented to stay for some time to bestow good fortune on the men and women of Mithilā.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतदेवोऽच्युतं प्राप्तं स्वगृहाञ्जनको यथा ।नत्वा मुनीन् सुसंहृष्टो धुन्वन् वासो ननर्त ह ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutadevo ’cyutaṁ prāptaṁsva-gṛhāñ janako yathānatvā munīn su-saṁhṛṣṭodhunvan vāso nanarta ha
verses.text: Śrutadeva received Lord Acyuta into his home with as much enthusiasm as that shown by King Bahulāśva. After bowing down to the Lord and the sages, Śrutadeva began to dance with great joy, waving his shawl.

verses.sanskrit: तृणपीठबृषीष्वेतानानीतेषूपवेश्य स: ।स्वागतेनाभिनन्द्याङ्‍‍घ्रीन् सभार्योऽवनिजे मुदा ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tṛṇa-pīṭha-bṛṣīṣv etānānīteṣūpaveśya saḥsvāgatenābhinandyāṅghrīnsa-bhāryo ’vanije mudā
verses.text: After bringing mats of grass and darbha straw and seating his guests upon them, he greeted them with words of welcome. Then he and his wife washed their feet with great pleasure.

verses.sanskrit: तदम्भसा महाभाग आत्मानं सगृहान्वयम् ।स्‍नापयां चक्र उद्धर्षो लब्धसर्वमनोरथ: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: tad-ambhasā mahā-bhāgaātmānaṁ sa-gṛhānvayamsnāpayāṁ cakra uddharṣolabdha-sarva-manorathaḥ
verses.text: With the wash water, the virtuous Śrutadeva copiously sprinkled himself, his house and his family. Overjoyed, he felt that all his desires had now been fulfilled.

verses.sanskrit: फलार्हणोशीरशिवामृताम्बुभि-र्मृदा सुरभ्या तुलसीकुशाम्बुजै: ।आराधयामास यथोपपन्नयासपर्यया सत्त्वविवर्धनान्धसा ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: phalārhaṇośīra-śivāmṛtāmbubhirmṛdā surabhyā tulasī-kuśāmbuyaiḥārādhayām āsa yathopapannayāsaparyayā sattva-vivardhanāndhasā
verses.text: He worshiped them with offerings of auspicious items easily available to him, such as fruits, uśīra root, pure, nectarean water, fragrant clay, tulasī leaves, kuśa grass and lotus flowers. Then he offered them food that increases the mode of goodness.

verses.sanskrit: स तर्कयामास कुतो ममान्वभूत्गृहान्धकूपे पतितस्य सङ्गम: ।य: सर्वतीर्थास्पदपादरेणुभि:कृष्णेन चास्यात्मनिकेतभूसुरै: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tarkayām āsa kuto mamānv abhūtgṛhāndha-kūpe patitasya saṅgamaḥyaḥ sarva-tīrthāspada-pāda-reṇubhiḥkṛṣṇena cāsyātma-niketa-bhūsuraiḥ
verses.text: He wondered: How is it that I, fallen into the blind well of family life, have been able to meet Lord Kṛṣṇa? And how have I also been allowed to meet these great brāhmaṇas, who always carry the Lord within their hearts? Indeed, the dust of their feet is the shelter of all holy places.

verses.sanskrit: सूपविष्टान् कृतातिथ्यान् श्रुतदेव उपस्थित: ।सभार्यस्वजनापत्य उवाचाङ्घ्र्यभिमर्शन: ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūpaviṣṭān kṛtātithyānśrutadeva upasthitaḥsa-bhārya-svajanāpatyauvācāṅghry-abhimarśanaḥ
verses.text: When his guests were seated comfortably, having each received a proper welcome, Śrutadeva approached them and sat down nearby with his wife, children and other dependents. Then, while massaging the Lord’s feet, he addressed Kṛṣṇa and the sages.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतदेव उवाचनाद्य नो दर्शनं प्राप्त: परं परमपूरुष: ।यर्हीदं शक्तिभि: सृष्ट्वा प्रविष्टो ह्यात्मसत्तया ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutadeva uvācanādya no darśanaṁ prāptaḥparaṁ parama-pūruṣaḥyarhīdaṁ śaktibhiḥ sṛṣṭvāpraviṣṭo hy ātma-sattayā
verses.text: Śrutadeva said: It is not that we have attained the audience of the Supreme Person only today, for we have in fact been associating with Him ever since He created this universe with His energies and then entered it in His transcendental form.

verses.sanskrit: यथा शयान: पुरुषो मनसैवात्ममायया ।सृष्ट्वा लोकं परं स्वाप्नमनुविश्यावभासते ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā śayānaḥ puruṣomanasaivātma-māyayāsṛṣṭvā lokaṁ paraṁ svāpnamanuviśyāvabhāsate
verses.text: The Lord is like a sleeping person who creates a separate world in his imagination and then enters his own dream and sees himself within it.

verses.sanskrit: श‍ृण्वतां गदतां शश्वदर्चतां त्वाभिवन्दताम् ।नृणां संवदतामन्तर्हृदि भास्यमलात्मनाम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śṛṇvatāṁ gadatāṁ śaśvadarcatāṁ tvābhivandatāmṇṛṇāṁ saṁvadatām antarhṛdi bhāsy amalātmanām
verses.text: You reveal Yourself within the hearts of those persons of pure consciousness who constantly hear about You, chant about You, worship You, glorify You and converse with one another about You.

verses.sanskrit: हृदिस्थोऽप्यतिदूरस्थ: कर्मविक्षिप्तचेतसाम् ।आत्मशक्तिभिरग्राह्योऽप्यन्त्युपेतगुणात्मनाम् ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: hṛdi-stho ’py ati-dūra-sthaḥkarma-vikṣipta-cetasāmātma-śaktibhir agrāhyo’py anty upeta-guṇātmanām
verses.text: But although You reside within the heart, You are very far away from those whose minds are disturbed by their entanglement in material work. Indeed, no one can grasp You by his material powers, for You reveal Yourself only in the hearts of those who have learned to appreciate Your transcendental qualities.

verses.sanskrit: नमोऽस्तु तेऽध्यात्मविदां परात्मनेअनात्मने स्वात्मविभक्तमृत्यवे ।सकारणाकारणलिङ्गमीयुषेस्वमाययासंवृतरुद्धद‍ृष्टये ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: namo ’stu te ’dhyātma-vidāṁ parātmaneanātmane svātma-vibhakta-mṛtyavesa-kāraṇākāraṇa-liṅgam īyuṣesva-māyayāsaṁvṛta-ruddha-dṛṣṭaye
verses.text: Let me offer my obeisances unto You. You are realized as the Supreme Soul by those who know the Absolute Truth, whereas in Your form of time You impose death upon the forgetful souls. You appear both in Your causeless spiritual form and in the created form of this universe, thus simultaneously uncovering the eyes of Your devotees and obstructing the vision of the nondevotees.

verses.sanskrit: स त्वं शाधि स्वभृत्यान् न: किं देव करवाम हे ।एतदन्तो नृणां क्लेशो यद् भवानक्षिगोचर: ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tvaṁ śādhi sva-bhṛtyān naḥkiṁ deva karavāma heetad-anto nṛṇāṁ kleśoyad bhavān akṣi-gocaraḥ
verses.text: O Lord, You are that Supreme Soul, and we are Your servants. How shall we serve You? My Lord, simply seeing You puts an end to all the troubles of human life.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचतदुक्तमित्युपाकर्ण्य भगवान् प्रणतार्तिहा ।गृहीत्वा पाणिना पाणिं प्रहसंस्तमुवाच ह ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācatad-uktam ity upākarṇyabhagavān praṇatārti-hāgṛhītvā pāṇinā pāṇiṁprahasaṁs tam uvāca ha
verses.text: Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After hearing Śrutadeva speak these words, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who relieves His surrendered devotees’ distress, took Śrutadeva’s hand in His own and, smiling, spoke to him as follows.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाचब्रह्मंस्तेऽनुग्रहार्थाय सम्प्राप्तान् विद्ध्यमून् मुनीन् ।सञ्चरन्ति मया लोकान् पुनन्त: पादरेणुभि: ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvācabrahmaṁs te ’nugrahārthāyasamprāptān viddhy amūn munīnsañcaranti mayā lokānpunantaḥ pāda-reṇubhiḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord said: My dear brāhmaṇa, you should know that these great sages have come here just to bless you. They travel throughout the worlds with Me, purifying them with the dust of their feet.

verses.sanskrit: देवा: क्षेत्राणि तीर्थानि दर्शनस्पर्शनार्चनै: ।शनै: पुनन्ति कालेन तदप्यर्हत्तमेक्षया ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: devāḥ kṣetrāṇi tīrthānidarśana-sparśanārcanaiḥśanaiḥ punanti kālenatad apy arhattamekṣayā
verses.text: One can gradually become purified by seeing, touching and worshiping temple deities, places of pilgrimage and holy rivers. But one can attain the same result immediately simply by receiving the glance of exalted sages.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणो जन्मना श्रेयान् सर्वेषां प्राणिनामिह ।तपसा विद्यया तुष्‍ट्या किमु मत्कलया युत: ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: brāhmaṇo janmanā śreyānsarveṣām prāṇinām ihatapasā vidyayā tuṣṭyākim u mat-kalayā yutaḥ
verses.text: By his very birth, a brāhmaṇa is the best of all living beings in this world, and he becomes even more exalted when he is endowed with austerity, learning and self-satisfaction, what to speak of devotion to Me.

verses.sanskrit: न ब्राह्मणान्मे दयितं रूपमेतच्चतुर्भुजम् ।सर्ववेदमयो विप्र: सर्वदेवमयो ह्यहम् ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: na brāhmaṇān me dayitaṁrūpam etac catur-bhujamsarva-veda-mayo vipraḥsarva-deva-mayo hy aham
verses.text: Even My own four-armed form is no dearer to Me than a brāhmaṇa. Within himself a learned brāhmaṇa comprises all the Vedas, just as within Myself I comprise all the demigods.

verses.sanskrit: दुष्प्रज्ञा अविदित्वैवमवजानन्त्यसूयव: ।गुरुं मां विप्रमात्मानमर्चादाविज्यद‍ृष्टय: ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: duṣprajñā aviditvaivamavajānanty asūyavaḥguruṁ māṁ vipram ātmānamarcādāv ijya-dṛṣṭayaḥ
verses.text: Ignorant of this truth, foolish people neglect and enviously offend a learned brāhmaṇa, who, being nondifferent from Me, is their spiritual master and very self. They consider worshipable only such obvious manifestations of divinity as My Deity form.

verses.sanskrit: चराचरमिदं विश्वं भावा ये चास्य हेतव: ।मद्रूपाणीति चेतस्याधत्ते विप्रो मदीक्षया ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: carācaram idaṁ viśvaṁbhāvā ye cāsya hetavaḥmad-rūpāṇīti cetasyādhatte vipro mad-īkṣayā
verses.text: Because he has realized Me, a brāhmaṇa is firmly fixed in the knowledge that everything moving and nonmoving in the universe, and also the primary elements of its creation, are all manifest forms expanded from Me.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माद् ब्रह्मऋषीनेतान् ब्रह्मन् मच्छ्रद्धयार्चय ।एवं चेदर्चितोऽस्म्यद्धा नान्यथा भूरिभूतिभि: ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād brahma-ṛṣīn etānbrahman mac-chraddhayārcayaevaṁ ced arcito ’smy addhānānyathā bhūri-bhūtibhiḥ
verses.text: Therefore you should worship these brāhmaṇa sages, O brāhmaṇa, with the same faith you have in Me. If you do so, you will worship Me directly, which you cannot do otherwise, even with offerings of vast riches.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचस इत्थं प्रभुनादिष्ट: सहकृष्णान् द्विजोत्तमान् ।आराध्यैकात्मभावेन मैथिलश्चाप सद्गतिम् ॥ ५८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācasa itthaṁ prabhunādiṣṭaḥsaha-kṛṣṇān dvijottamānārādhyaikātma-bhāvenamaithilaś cāpa sad-gatim
verses.text: Śrī Śuka said: So instructed by his Lord, with single-minded devotion Śrutadeva worshiped Śrī Kṛṣṇa and the topmost brāhmaṇas accompanying Him, and King Bahulāśva did the same. Thus both Śrutadeva and the King attained the ultimate transcendental destination.

verses.sanskrit: एवं स्वभक्तयो राजन् भगवान् भक्तभक्तिमान् ।उषित्वादिश्य सन्मार्गं पुनर्द्वारवतीमगात् ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ sva-bhaktayo rājanbhagavān bhakta-bhaktimānuṣitvādiśya san-mārgaṁpunar dvāravatīm agāt
verses.text: O King, thus the Personality of Godhead, who is devoted to His own devotees, stayed for some time with His two great devotees Śrutadeva and Bahulāśva, teaching them the behavior of perfect saints. Then the Lord returned to Dvārakā.
